name: Tech Duinn
description: Tech Duinn, meaning "House of Donn," is a significant location in Irish mythology, representing the dwelling place of Donn, a god of the dead and ancestor of the Gaels. It is often envisioned as an island or a rocky outcrop off the southwestern coast of Ireland, specifically associated with Dursey Island. In mythological accounts, Tech Duinn serves as the point of departure for the souls of the dead on their journey to the Otherworld. Upon death, the souls would gather at Tech Duinn before embarking on their final voyage. Donn, residing in his house, would greet and guide the deceased, ensuring their passage to the afterlife. The location is imbued with a sense of solemnity and finality, marking the transition between the mortal world and the realm of spirits. While not a place of punishment, it is a place of reckoning and departure. The roaring of the ocean surrounding Tech Duinn is often interpreted as the lamentations of the dead or the voices of the spirits. The association of Donn with storms and the sea further reinforces the powerful and sometimes turbulent nature of this liminal space. It represents the inevitable journey that all mortals must eventually undertake, and the importance of respecting the cycle of life and death within Celtic cosmology. It is a place of power, fear, and ultimately, acceptance of fate.
subtitle: House of Donn, Abode of the Dead

keyMyths.title: The Gathering of Souls
keyMyths.description: When a person dies, their soul travels to Tech Duinn to prepare for the journey to the Otherworld. Donn, the lord of the dead, awaits them to guide them on their final voyage.

keyMyths.title: Donn's Role
keyMyths.description: Donn's role is not to judge, but to shepherd the dead. He facilitates their passage, ensuring they are ready for the next stage of their existence.

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of Irish mythological figures and locations.
